429.message: You are doing that too fast. Please wait a moment.

500.title: Server Error
500.message: Our servers encountered an issue. Please try again later.

study_not_found.title: Study Not Found
study_not_found.message: We couldn't find a study with that name. Please check the URL or contact the researcher.

draft.title: Study Under Preparation
draft.message: This study is not available for participation yet. Please check back later.

paused.title: Maintenance in Progress
paused.message: Data collection is temporarily paused for maintenance. We will be back shortly.

closed.title: Data Collection Closed
closed.message: This study is now closed. Participation is no longer possible.

layout.title: Open-Q
layout.pilot_mode: Pilot Mode: Changes are temporary & data will not be saved

consent.label: I have read the above information and agree to participate in this study.
consent.error: Please accept the terms to continue.

welcome.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to start a new session? This will clear your current progress.
welcome.default_instructions:
1. **Let's meet:** a few quick questions to better understand your background.
2. **First impressions:** discover the statements and give your immediate reaction (agree, neutral, or disagree).
3. **Your perspective:** refine your thoughts by placing each statement on the grid to reflect your exact viewpoint.
4. **Why:** a few words to explain your most significant choices.

profile.title: Let's meet
profile.description: A few quick questions to better understand your background.

rough.title: First impressions
rough.description: Discover the statements and give your immediate reaction (agree, neutral, or disagree).

fine.title: Your perspective
fine.description: Place the statements onto the grid to refine your point of view, prioritizing what matters most to you.

post.title: Why
post.description: A few words to explain your most significant choices.

contact.title: Results & Contact
contact.interview_consent: I agree to be potentially contacted for a follow-up interview.
contact.newsletter_consent: I wish to be informed about the study results.
contact.gdpr_note: Your email will never be publicly shared. It is stored solely for the purposes selected above.

consent.title: Participation Consent
consent.subtitle: Please read the following information carefully.
consent.default_text:
**Confirmation of understanding:** By checking the box below, you confirm that you have read the study objectives and procedure described on the welcome page. You understand that this task involves sorting statements to model your personal point of view and that there are no right or wrong answers.

**Confidentiality and data protection:** Your participation is anonymous. Your responses will be assigned a code and will not be linked to your identity in any reports or publications. The data collected will be analyzed collectively to identify shared perspectives (factors). Any written comments provided during the post-sort phase may be quoted anonymously to illustrate these viewpoints.

**Voluntary participation:** Participation is entirely voluntary. You reserve the right to stop the sorting process and close the browser at any time without penalty. If you withdraw before submission, your partial data will not be retained.

presort.title: About You
presort.description: Please answer a few questions to help us situate your viewpoint.

header.subtitle: Swipe **left** to Disagree, **right** to Agree, or **down** to be Neutral.
header.hint: Trust your **instinct**. This is spontaneous — you can change your mind right after.

complete.title: First Step Complete
complete.subtitle: We will now refine the nuances.

instructions.desktop_tip: Drag, press, or use keyboard ( ← ↓ → ) to sort

deck.title: Available Statements
deck.all_placed: All statements in this pile are sorted

workbench.drag_or_tap: Drag to grid or tap slot

methodology.extremes: Tip: Start with what is most obvious to you (the extremes).
methodology.vertical: In the same column, the order from top to bottom doesn't matter.
methodology.constraint: You have to fit everything! It can be a puzzle, but that's how we prioritize choices.
methodology.relative: Reminder: items are sorted relative to each other (relative agreement).
methodology.zoom: Feel free to zoom in on different parts of the grid for better visibility.
methodology.flexibility: Nothing is final: you can move cards or put them back in the deck at any time.

post.title: To conclude
post.description: You're almost done! Your comments on the most significant statements are crucial for our analysis.

extreme.why: Why did you choose to place this statement here? How is it significant to you?
extreme.placeholder: E.g., A specific event, a strong belief, or a particular word in the statement...
extreme.min_chars: A few words are enough to help us understand the context.

optional.title: Additional Comments (optional)
optional.description: Feel free to add comments to any other statement if you wish to elaborate on your choices.

post.validation_error: Please briefly explain your key choices to continue.

success.title: Thank you for your participation!
success.message: Your contribution to the study has been saved.

sidebar.select_study_msg: Please select or create a study to begin.

create.title: Create Workspace
create.description: Create a new organization to manage your studies and team.
create.card_description: Enter the name and URL slug for your new workspace.
create.url_description: This will be used in your workspace URL.

recruitment.title: Recruitment
recruitment.description: Generate secure links for your participants and monitor real-time recruitment efficiency.
recruitment.create_description: Target specific cohorts or create general access. Each link type has different security and usage properties.
recruitment.name_placeholder: e.g. LinkedIn, Email Batch A, Student List...
recruitment.qr_desc: Participants can scan this code to leap directly into the study.
recruitment.table_description: Active channels and link lifecycle management.
recruitment.share_desc: Distribute your study URL to invite participants.
recruitment.qr_print_hint: Scan or print this code for physical recruitment materials (flyers, posters).
recruitment.analytics_desc: Direct links and QR scans are automatically tracked in the Health Dashboard above.

guidance.public: Best for high-volume recruitment. A single URL for all participants.
guidance.individual: Maximum security. Generates unique links that expire after one successful completion.
guidance.limited: Controlled access. One link that stops working after a fixed number of submissions.

dashboard.title: Workspace Dashboard
dashboard.snapshot: Here's a snapshot of your research activity.
dashboard.recent_description: A list of your most recently updated studies.
dashboard.no_studies: No studies found. Create your first study to get started!

create_study.title: Create New Study
create_study.description: Start a new Q-Methodology study. You can configure statements and settings later.

validation.slug_min: Slug must be at least 3 characters
validation.slug_regex: Slug must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens

study_overview.title: Overview
study_overview.subtitle: Real-time analytics and participant overview for this study.
study_overview.view_all: View all participants and data details

draft.title: Revert to Draft?
draft.desc: This will stop data collection but allow you to modify the study design. Existing data is preserved.

active.title: Launch Study?
active.desc: Activating the study will open it to participants.

paused.title: Pause Study?
paused.desc: Participants will not be able to enter the study while it is paused. You can resume later.

closed.title: Close Study?
closed.desc: This will prevent new participants from entering. Existing sessions can still finish. You can reopen later.

team.title: Research Team
team.description: Manage collaborators and study-level access control.
team.invite_description: Generate a secure link to invite a new member to this study.
team.share_label: Share this link with the invitee:

permissions.owner: Can delete the study and manage team.
permissions.editor: Can modify design and view results.

design.translation_needed_desc: This content was automatically copied from another language. Please review and localize it.

guidance.intro_desc: Start by defining the purpose of your study. This information will be shown to participants before they begin the sorting process.
guidance.qsort_desc: Ensure your grid capacity exactly matches the number of statements. A balanced Q-set usually has between 30 and 60 items for robust factor analysis.

languages.manage_desc: Enable or disable languages available for participants to see in this study.
languages.auto_translate_desc: Soon you will be able to use AI to automatically translate your study into 30+ languages.
languages.activate_desc: Choose a source language to copy translations from. This prevents empty fields for participants.
languages.copy_notice: All fields will be copied. You will see a visual indicator on fields that still need your translation.

process_steps.title: Study Steps
process_steps.desc: Define the phases shown to participants on the welcome page. If left empty, default steps will be used.

empty.title: No custom steps defined
empty.desc: The study will use the standard Q-methodology workflow (Meet, First impressions, Perspective, Why).

defaults.new_desc: Explain what the participant will do in this phase.

fields.title: Study title
fields.subtitle: Subtitle (optional)
fields.objective_placeholder: What is the research question or purpose of this study?
fields.task_placeholder: Briefly explain the study process to participants (optional)...
fields.legal_placeholder: Enter the full legal agreement text...

intro.consent_desc: Participants must agree to these terms before starting.

condition.title: Condition of Instruction
condition.label: The Research Question
condition.desc: This is the core prompt that guides participants throughout the sorting process.
condition.placeholder: e.g., What is your stance on these statements?
condition.pre_desc: Provide more context or detailed instructions before the sorting task starts.

tips.title: Why is this important?
tips.desc: In Q-methodology, the 'Condition of Instruction' determines the point of view from which the participant sorts the items. It should be clear, singular, and consistent across all sorting phases.

questions.enable_presort_desc: Collect demographic information or other data before the sorting task.

empty.title: No questions yet
empty.desc: Click above to add your first question

bulk.title: Bulk editor (quick paste)
bulk.desc: One statement per line. Supports "Code: Text" format.
bulk.placeholder:
Paste your statements here...
1. First statement
2. Second statement

set.title: Q-set
set.confirm_clear: Are you sure you want to delete ALL statements? This cannot be undone.

settings.title: Research settings
settings.desc: Configure how statements are presented to participants
settings.show_codes_desc: Display statement codes (e.g., "S1", "S2") alongside the text.

grid.title: Forced distribution grid
grid.desc: The grid shape forces participants to discriminate between statements, approximating a normal distribution.
grid.tooltip_desc: Q-methodology uses a quasi-normal distribution (kurtosis > 0) to ensure participants make meaningful distinctions. The pyramid shape prevents central tendency bias.
grid.locked_desc: The grid and statements cannot be modified while the study is active/closed. This protects data integrity.
grid.mismatch_desc: You have {{statements}} statements but the grid only has slots for {{slots}} items. Please adjust the columns below to match.

extreme.title: Extreme columns
extreme.desc: Select columns that trigger follow-up questions.
extreme.prompt_placeholder: Why did you place this statement here?

random_comments.title: Allow random comments
random_comments.desc: Allow participants to add comments to any statement in the grid.

custom.title: Custom questions
custom.desc: Add custom questions to the post-sort survey.

email.title: Participant Follow-up
email.desc: Collect email addresses for follow-up or research results.
email.interview_warning: Note: agreeing to be contacted lifts anonymity for the researcher.
email.results_hint: Email solely for results, anonymity preserved.

interface.title: Interface customization
interface.desc: Customize the buttons and labels of the interface to match your study's tone (e.g., changing "Agree/Disagree" to "Like/Dislike").

terms.title: Sorting terminology
terms.desc: Define the labels for the spontaneous sort and the grid spectrum.

accent.title: Accent color
accent.desc: This color will be used for buttons, links, and highlights throughout the study.

logo.title: Study logo
logo.desc: Upload a custom logo to replace the default Open-Q branding.
logo.label: Logo URL
logo.hint: Recommended size: 200x50px. Supports PNG, SVG, JPG.

tip.title: Branding tip
tip.desc: Ensuring high accessibility is key. When picking an accent color, make sure it has enough contrast against white backgrounds for readability.

partners.title: Institutional Partners
partners.desc: Add logos of supporting institutions or partners.

data.title: Analytics & Data
data.description: Inspect participant performance, qualitative feedback, and export datasets.

csv.title: Universal CSV
csv.desc: Raw rectangular data. Best for R, Python, SPSS, or Excel analysis. Includes all metadata.

json.title: KenQ JSON
json.desc: Optimized for the Web-KenQ analysis tool. Contains study definition and sorts.

zip.title: PQMethod Bundle (ZIP)
zip.desc: Contains legacy .DAT and .STA files for DOS PQMethod analysis.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load dataset. Please try refreshing the page.

survey.title: Survey Responses
survey.csv_note: Full survey answers available in CSV export.
